name: Movies & TV
description: Test your knowledge of cinema, TV, and Hollywood legends.

questions.text: Who directed the 1975 film 'Jaws'?
questions.explanation: Steven Spielberg directed Jaws, which became the first modern blockbuster.

questions.text: Which film won the first Academy Award for Best Picture?
options: Sunrise: A Song of Two Humans
questions.explanation: Wings (1927) won the first Academy Award for Outstanding Picture at the inaugural ceremony in 1929.

questions.text: Who played Tony Stark / Iron Man in the Marvel Cinematic Universe?
questions.explanation: Robert Downey Jr. portrayed Tony Stark / Iron Man starting with the 2008 film Iron Man.

questions.text: What is the highest-grossing film of all time (not adjusted for inflation)?
questions.explanation: Avatar (2009, re-released 2022) holds the all-time box office record at over $2.9 billion worldwide.

questions.text: In 'The Godfather', what animal's head is placed in a character's bed?
questions.explanation: A severed horse head is placed in Hollywood producer Jack Woltz's bed as a mob warning.

questions.text: Which actress has won the most Academy Awards for Best Actress?
questions.explanation: Katharine Hepburn holds the record with four Best Actress Oscars (1933, 1967, 1968, 1981).

questions.text: Which TV show features the fictional paper company Dunder Mifflin?
questions.explanation: The Office (US) is set at the Scranton, Pennsylvania branch of Dunder Mifflin Paper Company.

questions.text: What year was the original Star Wars film released?
questions.explanation: Star Wars: A New Hope was released on May 25, 1977, and became a cultural phenomenon.

questions.text: Who played Hannibal Lecter in 'The Silence of the Lambs' (1991)?
questions.explanation: Anthony Hopkins won the Academy Award for Best Actor for his iconic portrayal of Hannibal Lecter.

questions.text: Which director is known for films such as 'Pulp Fiction' and 'Kill Bill'?
questions.explanation: Quentin Tarantino is renowned for his nonlinear storytelling and stylized violence across these films.

questions.text: What was the name of the TV series set in the fictional Westeros?
questions.explanation: Game of Thrones (2011-2019) on HBO was adapted from George R.R. Martin's A Song of Ice and Fire series.

questions.text: Which film holds the record for most Academy Award wins (tied)?
options: The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King
questions.explanation: Ben-Hur (1959), Titanic (1997), and The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King (2003) each won 11 Oscars.

questions.text: In 'Breaking Bad', what is Walter White's chemistry teacher alias?
questions.explanation: Walter White adopts the alias Heisenberg, a reference to physicist Werner Heisenberg's uncertainty principle.

questions.text: Who directed 'Schindler's List' (1993)?
questions.explanation: Steven Spielberg directed Schindler's List, which won seven Academy Awards including Best Picture and Best Director.

questions.text: Which classic line is from 'Casablanca' (1942)?
questions.explanation: 'Here's looking at you, kid.' is said by Humphrey Bogart's character Rick Blaine in Casablanca.

questions.text: What streaming service originally produced 'House of Cards' (US version)?
questions.explanation: Netflix debuted House of Cards in 2013 as its first major original scripted series.

questions.text: In which film does the line 'I see dead people' appear?
questions.explanation: Cole Sear (Haley Joel Osment) delivers this famous line in M. Night Shyamalan's The Sixth Sense (1999).

questions.text: Which actor played Forrest Gump?
questions.explanation: Tom Hanks won his second consecutive Best Actor Oscar for playing Forrest Gump (1994).

questions.text: What is the name of the fictional kingdom in 'Frozen'?
questions.explanation: Arendelle is the Scandinavian-inspired fictional kingdom where Elsa and Anna live in Frozen.

questions.text: Who composed the iconic score for 'Star Wars'?
questions.explanation: John Williams composed the Star Wars score, one of the most recognizable film soundtracks ever.

questions.text: Which film is set almost entirely on a single lifeboat in shark-infested waters?
questions.explanation: Alfred Hitchcock's Lifeboat (1944) takes place almost entirely within a lifeboat in the Atlantic Ocean.

questions.text: What was the first feature-length animated film produced by Walt Disney?
options: Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs
questions.explanation: Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs (1937) was Disney's—and the world's—first feature-length cel-animated film.

questions.text: Which TV show coined the term 'jumping the shark'?
questions.explanation: The phrase comes from a Happy Days episode where Fonzie literally jumps over a shark on water skis.

questions.text: In 'The Dark Knight' (2008), who played the Joker?
questions.explanation: Heath Ledger won a posthumous Academy Award for Best Supporting Actor for his portrayal of the Joker.

questions.text: Which film features the fictional African nation of Wakanda?
questions.explanation: Black Panther (2018) is set primarily in Wakanda, the technologically advanced fictional African nation.

questions.text: Who directed '2001: A Space Odyssey' (1968)?
questions.explanation: Stanley Kubrick directed 2001: A Space Odyssey, widely considered one of the greatest films ever made.

questions.text: What is the name of the dragon in 'How to Train Your Dragon'?
questions.explanation: Toothless is the Night Fury dragon befriended by Hiccup in DreamWorks' How to Train Your Dragon.

questions.text: In 'Friends', what is the name of Ross's pet monkey?
questions.explanation: Marcel the capuchin monkey is Ross's exotic pet in the early seasons of Friends.

questions.text: Which actress played Clarice Starling in 'The Silence of the Lambs'?
questions.explanation: Jodie Foster won the Academy Award for Best Actress for her role as FBI trainee Clarice Starling.

questions.text: What year did the TV show 'Breaking Bad' premiere?
questions.explanation: Breaking Bad premiered on AMC on January 20, 2008, and ran for five seasons until 2013.

questions.text: Which film is based on the true story of the 2003 Boston Red Sox season?
questions.explanation: Fever Pitch (2005) was reworked to be set during the Red Sox's 2004 World Series-winning season.

questions.text: Who plays the lead role of Daenerys Targaryen in 'Game of Thrones'?
questions.explanation: Emilia Clarke portrayed Daenerys Targaryen, the Mother of Dragons, throughout all eight seasons.

questions.text: Which 1994 film features a briefcase whose contents are never revealed?
questions.explanation: In Pulp Fiction, the glowing briefcase (the MacGuffin) is never shown to the audience.

questions.text: What is the highest-rated TV show on IMDb (as of 2024)?
questions.explanation: Breaking Bad holds the top spot on IMDb's TV ratings with a 9.5/10 based on millions of votes.

questions.text: Who directed 'Citizen Kane' (1941)?
questions.explanation: Orson Welles both directed and starred in Citizen Kane, often cited as the greatest film ever made.

questions.text: In 'The Matrix', what color pill does Neo take to learn the truth?
questions.explanation: Neo takes the red pill offered by Morpheus, which reveals the truth about the simulated reality.

questions.text: Which actor has appeared in the most number of films that grossed over $1 billion?
questions.explanation: Samuel L. Jackson has appeared in more billion-dollar films than any other actor due to his prolific career.

questions.text: What is the name of the ship in 'Alien' (1979)?
questions.explanation: The USCSS Nostromo is the commercial towing spaceship in Ridley Scott's Alien.

questions.text: Which classic film introduced the phrase 'May the Force be with you'?
questions.explanation: The phrase first appeared in the original Star Wars: A New Hope (1977).

questions.text: What was Alfred Hitchcock's first sound film?
questions.explanation: Blackmail (1929) was Hitchcock's first sound film and is also considered the first British sound film.

questions.text: In which city is the TV show 'Seinfeld' primarily set?
questions.explanation: Seinfeld is set primarily in Manhattan, New York City, mirroring creator Jerry Seinfeld's real life.

questions.text: Which film won Best Picture at the 2020 Academy Awards?
options: Once Upon a Time in Hollywood
questions.explanation: Parasite (Bong Joon-ho) became the first non-English language film to win Best Picture at the Oscars.

questions.text: Who played Jack Dawson in 'Titanic' (1997)?
questions.explanation: Leonardo DiCaprio starred as Jack Dawson opposite Kate Winslet in James Cameron's Titanic.

questions.text: Which TV network aired 'The Sopranos'?
questions.explanation: The Sopranos aired on HBO from 1999 to 2007 and is widely credited with the prestige TV revolution.

questions.text: What is the real name of the character known as 'The Mandalorian' in the Disney+ series?
questions.explanation: The Mandalorian's real name is Din Djarin, revealed later in the series.

questions.text: Which film features the fictional band Spinal Tap?
questions.explanation: This Is Spinal Tap (1984) is Rob Reiner's mockumentary about the fictional British heavy metal band.

questions.text: Who plays the role of Walter White in 'Breaking Bad'?
questions.explanation: Bryan Cranston won four Emmy Awards for Outstanding Lead Actor for his portrayal of Walter White.

questions.text: What is the highest-grossing animated film of all time?
questions.explanation: The Lion King (2019 remake) grossed over $1.6 billion worldwide, making it the highest-grossing animated film.

questions.text: Which director made 'Mulholland Drive' (2001)?
questions.explanation: David Lynch directed Mulholland Drive, his neo-noir mystery set in the dream-like world of Hollywood.

questions.text: What TV show features a dragon-obsessed family known as House Targaryen?
questions.explanation: House of the Dragon is the Game of Thrones prequel series focused on the Targaryen civil war.
